title: The Volcano Signal
description: On pages 12–15, the volcano signal as usopp is inspired by the giants’ courage, while the crew’s separate groups are pulled deeper into Little Garden’s hazards.
scene: The Volcano Signal. Usopp is inspired by the giants’ courage, while the crew’s separate groups are pulled deeper into Little Garden’s hazards. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: We're running out of food onboard so if you see any animals that look to be edible, can you hunt it down and bring it back?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I'll go hunt for some animals that you'd never be able to kill yourself.

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Or did you just imply that you could hunt a bigger game than I can...?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: That's it! I chall- enge you at hunting!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Why does everyone on this ship have to be like that...

dialogue.speaker: Miss Valentine
dialogue.text: Whoever brings back the most kilograms of meat will be the winner!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: though in my case, it'll be tonnes of meat.

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: I know exactly how you feel, Nami. Don't cry, at least I'm by your side...

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Why can't you be a little more dependable?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: I feel like I've read this in a book before...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I'm sure I've heard of it...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: There's a claim that looks like a squad!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: An Ammonite if you ask me...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I know I read it pretty recently...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Why's there a sea king on land?

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on the volcano signal. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
